senses.gloss: mountain-slope with currents or current-beds, canyon, gorge

. (TA.) It is said in a trad.,
And a rain will come, in consequence of which the end of a water-course will not be impeded
]: meaning to denote its abundance, and that no place will be exempt from it. (TA.) And in a prov.,
Such a one will not impede the end of a water-course
]: (K, * TA:) applied to the abject and contemptible. (K.) And in another, (ISh,)
will not, trust in the flow of thy water-course

]: applied to him in whom one does not trust: (ISh, K:) i. e. I do not, or will not, trust in what thou sayest, and what thou adducest: characterizing the person as a liar. (ISh.) And in another, (IAar,)

body.text_ar: مَا اَخَافُ اـِلَّا مِنْ سَيْلِ تَلْعَتِى

body.text: I fear not save from the flow of my water-course
]: i. e., from the sons of my uncle, and my relations: (IAar, K:) for he who descends the water-course is in danger: if the torrent come, it sweeps him away. (IAar.)
